points: Figures are congruent when they share the exact same shape and identical dimensions, capable of complete superposition.
Two triangles are identically congruent specifically if they satisfy the defined SAS, ASA, AAS, SSS, or RHS analytical congruence criteria.
When triangles are proven congruent, their corresponding parts (sides and angles) mathematically match evenly, universally abbreviated as CPCT.
A triangle maintaining at least two completely equal sides acts functionally as an isosceles triangle.
In an isosceles triangle, the internal angles directly positioned opposite to the equal-length sides are proportionally equal.
Conversely, if any two interior angles of a triangle are quantifiably equal, the sides located precisely across from them reliably measure equal in length.
